title: eToro Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026)
meta_title: eToro Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026) | TradingToolsHub
meta_description: eToro Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026). eToro (4.0★, Free). Step-by-step setup, features walkthrough, and pro tips. Updated 2026.
excerpt: Complete guide to setting up and using eToro — from account creation to pro-level tips.
content:
What is eToro?

eToro is a regulated multi-asset brokerage and social trading platform founded in 2007 and headquartered in Tel Aviv, Israel, with global offices in London, New York, and Sydney. The platform combines commission-free trading across stocks, ETFs, crypto, forex, and commodities with a unique "CopyTrader" feature that lets users automatically replicate the trades of experienced investors in real time. With 35 million+ registered users, eToro operates the world's largest social trading community, making it ideal for beginner investors who want to learn by observing successful traders, passive investors seeking a set-and-forget approach, and multi-asset traders who want exposure to stocks, crypto, and forex from a single account. eToro's 4.0/5 rating reflects strong mobile experience and low barriers to entry, though it carries trade-offs around fees and regulatory restrictions depending on your location.

How to Create Your eToro Account

Creating an eToro account takes 10-15 minutes and requires minimal documentation for basic trading. Follow these steps:

  • Step 1: Visit eToro and Click "Join Now" — Go to the eToro website and select your country of residence. This matters because US users face regulatory restrictions on forex CFDs and leverage.
  • Step 2: Provide Basic Information — Enter your email, create a password, and agree to the terms. eToro will send you a verification email; click the link to confirm your address.
  • Step 3: Complete Personal Details — Fill in your full name, date of birth, address, and phone number. eToro uses this for regulatory compliance. If you live in the US, Canada, or certain other countries, some features will be restricted by law.
  • Step 4: Financial Information and Experience — eToro asks about your income, net worth, employment status, and trading experience. These questions help them classify your account and inform you of appropriate risk levels. Answer honestly; this isn't used to approve or reject you, but to set leverage limits.
  • Step 5: Identity Verification — eToro requires proof of identity (passport or driver's license) and proof of address (utility bill or bank statement dated within the last 3 months). Upload clear photos; blurry or cropped documents cause delays. Most users are verified within 24 hours, though it can take up to 5 business days during high-volume periods.
  • Step 6: Fund Your Account — Once verified, you can deposit funds. eToro accepts credit/debit cards, bank transfers, PayPal, and Skrill. The minimum deposit is typically $10-$200 depending on your payment method and region. Note: non-USD deposits incur currency conversion fees, a hidden cost not always obvious at checkout.
  • Step 7: Set Your Account Type — Choose between a Real Money account and a Demo (paper trading) account. The demo is ideal for exploring eToro's interface risk-free before trading real capital. You start with $100,000 in virtual funds.

Estimated Setup Time: 15-20 minutes for account creation; 24-48 hours for verification; then immediate access to paper trading, with real money trading available once verified.

Setting Up eToro for the First Time

Once your account is verified and funded, configure these settings before placing your first trade:

1. Explore the Dashboard Layout — eToro's main dashboard shows your portfolio balance, open positions, watchlist, and a real-time news feed. The left sidebar contains your portfolio, watchlist, and discover feed (where you find investors to copy). The top navigation includes your account settings, messages, and notifications. Spend 5 minutes clicking around to get oriented; the interface is intentionally mobile-first and minimal, so there's not much to learn.

2. Set Up Your Watchlist — Add assets you're interested in (stocks, ETFs, crypto, forex pairs) to your watchlist so you can track prices without buying immediately. Click the star icon on any asset to add it. eToro lets you group watchlists by category, which is useful if you track 20+ instruments.

3. Enable Mobile App Notifications — Download the eToro mobile app (iOS/Android) and log in with your credentials. Enable push notifications for price alerts and portfolio updates. Mobile is where eToro shines—the app is faster and more responsive than the web platform, and most users end up doing 70%+ of their trading on mobile.

4. Configure Your Risk Settings — Go to Account Settings → Risk Settings and set your maximum leverage tolerance and stop-loss defaults. eToro will warn you if you're approaching margin requirements, but you need to be proactive; most account liquidations happen because traders didn't understand their leverage exposure.

5. Review Fees and Withdrawal Settings — Check Account Settings → Withdrawals. Be aware that eToro charges a $5 flat fee on every withdrawal, regardless of amount. If you plan to withdraw frequently, these fees add up—a monthly withdrawal habit costs $60/year. Also note the $10/month inactivity fee kicks in after 12 months of no logins, so even inactive accounts bleed money.

6. Set Up Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) — Go to Account Settings → Security and enable 2FA via your phone. This is critical if you're trading with real money. eToro accounts with 2FA enabled are significantly harder to compromise.

Essential Features You Should Know

CopyTrader — The Core Differentiator — CopyTrader is eToro's flagship feature and the reason most new users join. Click on any investor's profile in the Discover feed, review their statistics (returns, trade frequency, sharpe ratio, max drawdown), and click "Copy." All their future trades automatically execute in your account proportionally based on how much capital you allocate to them. For example, if you allocate $100 to copy an investor, and they buy 10 shares of Apple, you'll buy shares proportionally. You can copy up to 100 investors simultaneously. This feature is powerful for passive investors but carries hidden risk—past performance doesn't guarantee future results, and some "top investors" are just lucky rather than skilled. Always review at least 2-3 years of history before copying.

Paper Trading (Demo Account) — eToro gives you a separate demo account with $100,000 in virtual funds. Use this to test trading strategies, practice using the mobile app, and explore assets without risking real money. The demo uses real market prices (with a 15-minute delay for stocks), so it's a genuine sandbox. Most traders should spend at least 1-2 weeks in the demo before funding a real account.

Alerts and Real-Time Notifications — Set price alerts on watchlisted assets so you get notified when a stock hits your target price or falls below your stop level. Alerts appear as push notifications on mobile and email notifications on desktop. eToro also shows price movement percentages, so you can quickly scan which positions are up or down. Unlike some brokers, alerts are not automated—they just notify you; you still need to place the trade manually.

Social Features and News Feed — The Discover feed shows what other users are trading, copying, and discussing. You can follow specific investors, see their portfolios, and engage in comments on their trades. The built-in news feed includes market-moving news, earnings alerts, and economic calendar events. This creates a community aspect that pure brokers like Interactive Brokers lack, making it useful for learning what other traders think about major moves.

Performance Analytics and Risk Management — Your portfolio page displays real-time P&L, portfolio composition, and exposure breakdowns by asset class (stocks, crypto, forex, etc.). Risk management tools include built-in stop-loss orders (which trigger automatic sales if an asset falls below a threshold) and take-profit orders (which close positions when you hit your target). eToro also shows your open leverage and margin usage. However, charting is limited—you can't add custom technical indicators or drawing tools, which frustrates advanced traders. For complex analysis, you'd need to use TradingView or another charting platform alongside eToro.

Popular Investor Program — If you're a successful trader, apply to become a Popular Investor. eToro pays you based on how many users copy you (starting at $200/month for modest copying, scaling to $5,000+/month for top performers). This creates a genuinely incentivized marketplace where skilled traders share their strategies. It's not passive income—your trades are scrutinized publicly—but it's a unique way to monetize trading skill.

eToro Pricing: Which Plan Should You Choose?

eToro's pricing is deceptively simple: all account types are free to open and maintain. There are three account tiers, but none charge a monthly subscription:

  • Standard Account ($0/month) — The default tier for most traders. No monthly fee, commission-free stock and ETF trading, access to all assets (stocks, crypto, forex, commodities), CopyTrader enabled, and paper trading included. This is sufficient for 90% of users.
  • eToro Club Silver ($0/month) — An aspirational tier that used to require $5,000 minimum balance but is now free. You get priority customer support, a dedicated account manager, and exclusive webinars. However, the benefit difference from Standard is minimal if you're a self-directed trader. Worthwhile only if you value having a support contact for account issues.
  • eToro Club Gold+ ($0/month) — The premium tier, also free but designed for VIP traders. Includes everything from Silver plus additional perks like invitations to exclusive events and slightly better withdrawal processing. Again, free, but the practical value is marginal.

Hidden Costs to Factor In: While account tiers are free, eToro has several fees that add up:

  • $5 flat fee per withdrawal (every withdrawal costs $5, regardless of size)
  • $10/month inactivity fee after 12 months of no logins
  • Currency conversion fees on non-USD deposits (typically 1-2%)
  • Overnight holding fees (overnight swap rates) on leveraged positions—typically 1-5% annualized, charged daily for forex and CFD positions held overnight
  • Spread costs—eToro doesn't charge commissions, but instruments have built-in spreads (the difference between buy and sell price) which is how they make money

Who Should Choose eToro? Beginners and passive investors benefit most from Standard accounts and CopyTrader. If you plan to withdraw monthly, the $5 fee makes eToro less attractive than Interactive Brokers, which has lower withdrawal costs. For crypto-focused traders, eToro competes with Coinbase, but eToro offers more assets (forex, commodities) in one account. If you're active in forex with US residency, the regulatory restrictions (no CFD leverage) make Plus500 a potential alternative, though Plus500 doesn't have CopyTrader.

Pro Tips for Getting the Most Out of eToro

1. Copy Multiple Investors, Not Just One Star — Avoid putting all your capital behind a single "top investor." Instead, allocate smaller amounts ($50-$100 each) across 5-10 investors with different strategies and track records. This diversifies your risk; if one investor hits a bad streak, your entire portfolio doesn't collapse. Also, review investors quarterly and remove underperformers.

2. Use the Demo Account as Your Real Education — Before depositing money, spend 30 days in the demo account testing CopyTrader. Follow 10 different investors and see which ones actually generate consistent returns. Most traders rush to real money too fast and end up copying investors who got lucky, not skilled.

3. Set Strict Withdrawal Discipline to Offset Fees — If you plan to withdraw profits regularly, do it quarterly or semi-annually instead of monthly. One $100 withdrawal costs $5 (5% fee); quarterly withdrawals of $400 cost only $5 (1.25% fee). Batching withdrawals saves hundreds per year.

4. Enable Stop-Loss on Every Position Over $1,000 — eToro makes stop-losses easy to set, but most traders skip them and lose big on unexpected market moves. Always set a stop-loss at entry—no exceptions. A 2% stop-loss on a $1,000 position costs you $20 max; ignoring a stop costs you $500+ if the trade goes wrong.

5. Monitor Overnight Swap Fees on Forex and CFD Positions — If you hold forex or CFD positions overnight, eToro charges interest (swap rates). A leveraged position held 5 nights a week incurs cumulative fees that erode returns. Check the swap rate before entering a position—it's listed in the "instrument info" panel. Positions with high swap fees (above 5% annualized) are expensive to hold long-term.

6. Avoid the Inactivity Fee by Setting Phone Reminders — The $10/month inactivity fee is punitive. If you have an account and aren't actively trading, set a reminder to log in once every 11 months. A single 2-minute login resets the clock and saves you $120/year.

7. Use Crypto for Dollar-Cost Averaging — eToro allows fractional crypto purchases from just $10, which is ideal for dollar-cost averaging (investing fixed amounts regularly). Unlike some crypto exchanges, you can set up recurring buys directly in the app, automating your strategy without manual trades.

Common eToro Issues and How to Fix Them

Issue 1: Copied Investor Stops Trading or Turns Negative — This happens unexpectedly. Solution: eToro provides performance data on all copied investors. Check their stats monthly and unfollow anyone whose returns drop below your threshold or who hasn't traded in 2+ weeks. Most successful copy traders unfollow 20% of their followed investors annually due to performance decay or investor inactivity.

Issue 2: Surprise Currency Conversion Fees on Deposits — If you deposit in a currency other than USD and your account is USD-based, eToro applies a conversion fee at checkout (usually 1-2%), which isn't always obvious. Solution: Deposit in your account's base currency when possible. If cross-currency deposits are unavoidable, wire transfers (bank transfers) have lower conversion fees than card deposits.

Issue 3: Basic Charting Limits Frustrate Technical Traders — eToro's charting tools lack custom indicators, volume profiles, and advanced drawing tools. If you need these, open a TradingView chart in a second tab and mirror your eToro trades there. Many experienced traders use eToro for execution but TradingView for analysis, since eToro doesn't support complex strategies like options spreads anyway.

Issue 4: Withdrawals Are Slow and Expensive — Processing withdrawals takes 1-3 business days, and the $5 flat fee applies even to small withdrawals. Solution: If you're dealing with small account balances ($100-$500), don't withdraw immediately—keep capital in your account or wait until you have larger sums to make the 5% fee proportionally smaller. For large withdrawals ($10,000+), the fee is negligible.

Is eToro Worth It? Our Verdict

eToro is worth using if you're a beginner or passive investor who values simplicity and the unique advantage of CopyTrader. The 4.0/5 rating reflects genuine strengths: a 35-million-user community, commission-free stock and ETF trading, and effortless access to crypto and forex from one app. However, the $5 withdrawal fees, $10/month inactivity penalties, and limited charting make it less suitable for active traders or those who frequently move money. If you plan to copy top investors and hold positions long-term, eToro is excellent; if you're a technical trader executing 10+ trades daily, you'll outgrow it quickly. Compare eToro against competitors like Interactive Brokers (for advanced traders) or Coinbase (for crypto-only traders) based on your strategy, but for most beginners starting with under $5,000, eToro remains the most intuitive entry point into multi-asset trading.
author: TradingToolsHub Editorial
